sections.paragraph:
ἀνόμοιος, ον (s. ὅμοιος; Pind., Pla. et al.; POxy 237 VI, 29; Wsd 2:15; Philo; Tat. 12:3; Ath. 8:1) unlike w. dat. (Pla., Gorg. 513b; Wsd) of a star πόθεν ἡ καινότης ἡ ἀ. αὐτοῖς whence the new thing, unlike them (the other stars), might come IEph 19:2.—DELG s.v. ὁμός.

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

dat. dat. = dative

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 85
